decision: By Friday 23 May, approve our strategy: notify warranty claims to Lockwood, pursue the hidden director's loan as a personal fraud claim, get an injunction on the non-compete, and reconcile the FY2026 earn-out before we issue.
rendered:
The decision: we need your sign-off by Friday 23 May on six items below.

Where we are. A year on from buying TideSync, four problems have surfaced that Lockwood did not disclose: (1) the Belmont contract — 22% of revenue — was already on notice to terminate when we signed; (2) HMRC had an open compliance check against TideSync's R&D claims worth £640,000; (3) a £180,000 director's loan to Lockwood was reclassified out of the completion accounts three days before completion with no supporting documentation; (4) the RouteCore software licence terminated on the change of control and was not consented.

All four are breaches of the warranties in the SPA. Our preliminary view of total recovery is £3.4m–£3.85m. The cap on warranty claims is £4m and we have Markel W&I insurance for £4m, so insurance should carry most of it.

One hard date: warranty claims must be notified by 14 August 2026. A late notification kills the claim regardless of how strong it is.

What we recommend. We notify the warranty claims through the insurance route — that's the cleanest recovery. We pursue the hidden director's loan as a personal fraud claim against Lockwood (uninsured but uncapped — and running fraud through the insured route would put the insurance at risk). And we get an injunction on Lockwood's competing business now, because every week he trades erodes the value of the restraint.

Lockwood will counterclaim on his earn-out (he says we manipulated FY2026 accounts) and on his consultancy termination. The earn-out point has independent substance — we should reconcile it with the forensic accountants before issuing.

What we need from you. Six authorisations are set out in paragraph 70 of the underlying memo. Indicative legal budget: £85,000–£120,000 to get the notifications out and the strategy locked.

claims.claim: Decision required by Friday 23 May 2026 on six items
Belmont Freight contract was 22% of revenue and was on notice to terminate when we signed
HMRC compliance check on R&D claims worth £640,000 was open before completion and not disclosed
£180,000 director's loan to Lockwood was reclassified out of the completion accounts three days before completion with no supporting documentation
The RouteCore software licence terminated on the change of control and consent was not obtained
All four are breaches of the SPA warranties
Warranty cap £4m and Markel W&I insurance £4m
Warranty claim notification deadline 14 August 2026; late notification kills the claim
Running fraud through the insured route would put the W&I insurance at risk because the policy excludes pre-completion seller fraud
Every week of permitted competing trading by Lockwood erodes the value of the non-compete restraint
Lockwood will counterclaim on earn-out manipulation and on consultancy termination
The earn-out counterclaim has independent substance and should be reconciled before we issue
Indicative legal budget £85,000–£120,000 for Phase 1 notifications and strategy lock
